summary: work_chunks mapping verified correct: id 1->Bead1(4ft2,R1+R2), id 2->Bead2(3cfr,R3), id 3->Bead3(gpoq,R4), all depends_on:[] — positional order matches the ## Bead N section order exactly, so no wrong-dependency wiring. All cited line numbers verified against source: checkADRCitations L465, checkADRCoverage L516, adr-coverage-missing L529, ValidatePlan L73 with the empty-citations adr-citations WARN at L141/ERROR L143, divergence candidateDomains at L142, scaffoldPlan frontmatter L228-233 (status/spec_id/version, no adr_citations key — R4's gap is real), parse.go **Domain(s)** at L74 already accepts non-list form (R3 test is a pure lock, parser unchanged), adr.go adrListCmd L97 / adrShowCmd L138 both use FindRoot (R3's fix target). All named helpers exist (resolveDomains L211, listDomainDirs docsync L351, loadOwnershipForRef L221, matchesAny L269, GlobMatch L294, attributeDomain L250) in internal/validate, confirming the helper-placement justification (zero new exports). All test fixtures exist: writeSpecAndPlan, writeADR, writeManifest, writeTestSpec, writeTestADRWithDomains, makePlanWithCitations, setupWorktreePair. The L28-29 ownership multi-owner fixture confirmed (alpha+beta both claim internal/foo/**); NOTE attributeDomain returns FIRST-MATCH not error, so Bead1's helper must implement its own owner-collection for the >1-owner ERROR — the plan correctly specifies reusing loadOwnershipForRef+matchesAny directly to collect owners rather than delegating ambiguity to attributeDomain, so this is handled. One-cycle: Bead1 is large (new file + 2 gate wirings + R2 msg + ADR-0032 edit + ~9 tests) but cohesive (all hang off one normalization helper feeding gates that all live in internal/validate); the test-first RED->GREEN structure and explicit changed-file list make it tractable for one agent/one commit. Beads 2/3 are small. PARALLEL-SAFETY: Bead1 and Bead3 both edit internal/validate/plan.go but at genuinely disjoint regions — Bead1 at L124(loadImpactedDomains routing)/L465/L516/L529, Bead3 at L141-143 (empty-citations branch in ValidatePlan, a different function). These are non-adjacent hunks; mindspec merges beads sequentially into the spec branch (2nd merge rebases), so a hunk-level conflict is very unlikely and if one arose it would be a trivial rebase. The plan documents this as a merge-order preference (R1 first, R4 rebases) rather than a depends_on edge, which is correct per spec-097-R3 semantics: depends_on encodes runtime output-consumption, and no bead consumes another's runtime output here — a mere co-located-file preference does not warrant a serializing edge. All bead files resolve to the single workflow domain (internal/validate, internal/approve, internal/adr, cmd all in workflow OWNERSHIP.yaml paths), matching the ADR Fitness single-impacted-domain claim. No missing scaffolding. Gate commands per bead are present, correctly filtered, and honor the never-run-harness constraint. NOTE for Bead1 plan path: ValidatePlan(root,specID) has NO executor and loadImpactedDomains(specDir) takes only specDir — the plan flags this (use on-disk LoadOwnership/resolveDomains branch needing no executor for ownerRef==""), which is the correct resolution since resolveDomains/loadOwnershipForRef accept a nil exec for the working-tree on-disk path (the L32 ownership test calls attributeDomain(nil,...)). This is an advisory the implementing agent should heed, not a blocker.
